title: Modular Software Architecture and Algorithms for Fast and Efficient Lunar Mobility (M-SAFE)
description: ProtoInnovations, LLC proposes a software architecture for autonomous mobility solutions tailored to meet the demanding speed-made-good and reliability requirements during challenging semi-autonomous NASA lunar missions. Building upon ProtoInnovations's previous successful experience in developing reconfigurable and modular mobility software architectures and robotic systems for autonomous rover operations, we propose an optimized mobility software architecture for high-speed and high-cadence mi

title: Radiation hardness study of superconducting detectors and electronics
description: Often, electronics and sensors that operate well on earth cannot operate in space due to damage from the high levels of unavoidable radiation that exists there. Radiation shielding can be used to protect humans and some electronics, but sensors often must be exposed to the outside environment, and thus cannot be shielded. The ability of a sensor or electronic element to operate despite exposure to radiation is referred to as its "hardness." One technology of great utility on earth, but so far

title: Space Robotics Challenge - Autonomous Robotic Systems for Lunar ISRU
description: Advance robotic software that increases the autonomy of dexterous humanoid robots
technologyArea: Activity and Resource Planning and Scheduling

title: Development of A Novel Receiver for Coherent Communication
description: Optical communications through the atmosphere can be challenging due to the effects of atmospheric turbulence. To advance the technology readiness of this concept; we will procure and integrate a multi-channel integrated PIC receiver; develop the software codes; and perform a laboratory demonstration of real-time coherent combining in the laboratory environment. We will deliver hardware that can be directly integrated into a ground terminal to receive a space-to-ground communications link.

title: Spacesuit Cover against the Abrasive Lunar Environment (SCALE) & eXploration Textile for high Oxygen eNvironments (xTON)
description: NASA has entered a new era of human space exploration with the Artemis Program, hoping to establish a long-term human presence on the Moon. To meet NASA needs, new textiles will have to be developed that keep astronauts safe, while still enabling those astronauts to perform at their best. In the solicitation, NASA has identified two specific areas for textile innovation: (Part A) the Exploration Extravehicular Mobility Unit (xEMU) Environmental Protection Garment (EPG) and (Part B) crew cloth
partners: North Carolina State University at Raleigh

title: High Multi-Speed Time-Triggered Ethernet
description: LeWiz Phase 1 research showed strong needs for US based time-triggered Ethernet (TTE) solutions for use by US agencies and their contractors. TTE is an SAE AS6802 standard for aerospace applications. Current market leader is an Austrian company. NASA used 1Gbps foreign solution in Artemis projects and called for TTE development by US companies in its 2022 SBIR Z2.02 topic. The US Navy also has requested for US based TTE solution. In NASA Phase 1, we developed a TTE IP core - scalable to speed up

title: Mission-Aware Payloads for Unmanned Platforms
description: Sentix and Brigham Young University propose the research and development of embedded payload intelligence for inflight optimization of surveillance, reconnaissance, and scientific missions. The current proposal leverages a substantial body of scientific and experimental knowledge derived from the Tactical Seeability™ System developed by Sentix' staff and BYU researchers to provide fully automated, optimal optical sensing over rugged 3D terrain. Discriminating features of our target capability in

title: Radio Frequency Mass Gauging - Next Generation System Development
description: This project will develop an advanced Radio Frequency Mass Gauging (RFMG) system, designated as a NextGen configuration, that will incorporate a Software-Defined Radio architecture. The specific objectives for this project will be to design, build, and test RFMG NextGen Software Defined Radio controller and test it with the integrated RFMG antenna and tank system.

title: Miniaturized Radiation Hardened Beam-Steerable GPS Receiver Front End
description: Position, Navigation and Timing (PNT) capability via GPS services are used by NASA for (1) real-time on-board autonomous navigation, (2) attitude determination and (3) earth science including sea height and climate monitoring. It is expected that over the next two decades approximately 95% of ALL space missions will operate within the GPS service envelope. GPS receivers will be "embedded" in most instruments and will require improved SWAP and increased sensitivity for improved tolerance from lar

title: Avionics Intrusion Detection and Attack Identification
description: Cyber threat identification includes the ability to detect, track, and disrupt advanced persistent threats. While emerging avionics system architectures support limited cyber hygiene and rudimentary defense, well-tailored cyber-attacks remain elusive to current detection technology. Additionally, the inherent structure of avionics systems makes monitoring and detection difficult. To meet the NASA need, QED proposes the Cyber Overwatch tool that provides a host-based threat detection capability f

title: R5
description: Realizing Rapid, Reduced-cost high-Risk Research (R5) is a technology demonstration mission to build and operate rapid, low-cost, highly-capable spacecraft platforms to demonstrate payloads of interest and technology relevant to human spaceflight.

title: Propellant Gauging During On-Orbit Refueling and Transfer Operations
description: In-space propellant transfer and refueling is a critical-path technology for sustained human presence in space. Researchers at Carthage College are advancing Propellant Gauging During On-Orbit Refueling and Transfer Operations with Modal Propellant Gauging(MPG),which is a technique that translates measured modal frequencies and mode shapes for precise estimates of propellant quantity. Parabolic and suborbital flights will test the ability of MPG to gauge equilibrated liquids in microgravity d

title: "Swarming Proxima Centauri: Coherent Picospacecraft Swarms Over Interstellar Distances"
description: Tiny gram-scale interstellar probes pushed by laser light are likely to be the only technology capable of reaching another star this century. We presuppose availability by mid-century of a laser beamer powerful enough (~100-GW) to boost a few grams to relativistic speed, lasersails robust enough to survive launch, and terrestrial light buckets (~1-sq.km) big enough to catch our optical signals. Then our proposed representative mission, around the third quarter of this century, is to fly by ou

title: Integrated Brayton Cryocooler for LOx and LH2 Applications
description: NASA is in need of affordable and robust cryogenic cooling solutions for use in space applications. In support of the Artemis program, NASA seeks innovative integrated refrigeration cycles for use in liquefaction of hydrogen and oxygen from the lunar surface. Based on initial estimates from Phase I activities, 330+ W of cooling is needed at 90 K and 130+ W of cooling is needed at 20 K to support at least 11.7 metric tons per year. Currently, space-based cryocoolers have yet to demonstrate coolin
technologyArea: Thermal Conditioning for Sensors, Instruments, Samples, and High-Efficiency Electric Motors

title: Frequency Agile Mid-IR Source for Planetary Exploration
description: The Small Business Innovative Research Phase I proposal seeks to develop a compact, room-temperature widely tunable middle infrared laser source that will be ideal for detecting methane, it's isotopes and related species using ultrasensitive absorption spectroscopies. The broad tuning ability of the laser will also enable the detection of multiple gas-phase trace species in planetary atmospheres with high selectivity. The laser source will be engineered to be ideally suited for use with high fin

title: Efficient Uncertainty Quantification for Intelligent Autonomous Systems
description: Linear Covariance Analysis (LCA) is a computationally efficient method for uncertainty quantification that yields direct statistical information about a nonlinear trajectory. LCA performs three orders of magnitude faster than the current method for uncertainty quantification, Monte Carlo Analysis (MCA), and yields direct statistical measures of uncertainty. Currently, uncertainty can only be propagated about a single reference trajectory in LCA, LCA can only model Gaussian noise, and LCA can onl

title: Deep Space PNT Instrument
description: This effort will mature a sensor for small satellite positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) in deep space using signals emitted by celestial x-ray sources (pulsars, asynchronous x-ray sources). The sensor records the arrival times of x-ray signals from a distant source to the CubeSat and estimates the distance between the CubeSat and the signal's coordinate system. A single pulsar measurement provides a rough distance estimate, and this can be refined using additional data sources like tra
